description: Web app (apps/web): fix the 'black theme' (dark mode) defects. The dark theme is wired via next-themes (class strategy) plus a large hand-written override layer in app/globals.css. There are concrete bugs: a broken 'System' theme option, fragile blanket `.dark .bg-white{...}` overrides that recolor elements meant to stay white (e.g. toggle knobs), missing dark variant for text selection, three inconsistent theme-toggle components, and several components with no dark styling. Independent of the staff-app features.
steps: Resolve the System-theme inconsistency: apps/web/components/providers.tsx sets ThemeProvider enableSystem={false}, but apps/web/components/account/settings-client.tsx renders a 'System' option calling setTheme('system'). Either (a) enable system support (enableSystem, and use resolvedTheme where the resolved value matters) OR (b) remove the 'System' ThemeOption and make the toggle strictly light/dark. Pick one and make it fully consistent across the settings client and all toggles. Document the choice in findings.

Fix fragile global overrides in apps/web/app/globals.css: the blanket rules `.dark .bg-white`, `.dark .bg-slate-50`, `.dark .text-slate-900/800/700`, `.dark .border-slate-100/200` recolor EVERY matching element in dark mode, including ones that must stay white/light (e.g. the SettingToggle knob `bg-white shadow-sm`, badges, small dots). Replace this pattern: prefer converting the affected components to semantic tokens (bg-card/bg-background/text-foreground/border-border) or explicit dark: variants, then narrow or remove the blanket overrides so intentional white/light elements are preserved. Verify the settings toggle knob and any status dots still render light-on-dark correctly.

Add a dark-mode `::selection` rule in globals.css (the current `::selection { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.12) }` is nearly invisible on dark backgrounds); provide a `.dark ::selection` with adequate contrast.

Consolidate the three duplicate theme toggles (components/theme-toggle.tsx, components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx, components/ui/theme-toggle-inline.tsx) into a single canonical implementation (or clearly-scoped variants sharing one hook usage). Make them consistent about theme vs resolvedTheme, ensure the mounted placeholder is dark-aware (the inline placeholder currently uses bg-slate-100 with no dark variant), and update all import sites.

Add dark styling to the components/pages flagged as using bg-white (or other light-only colors) with NO dark: variants: components/language-switcher.tsx, components/web-onboarding.tsx, components/admin/new-order-alert.tsx, components/admin/collections-client.tsx, components/admin/customer-requests-client.tsx, app/global-error.tsx, and audit app/dashboard/page.tsx (raw bg-black hero) and app/admin/dispatch/page.tsx / app/admin/orders/[id]/page.tsx for dark correctness. Use semantic tokens or dark: variants consistent with the rest of the app.

Sanity-check that dark mode reads correctly on the main surfaces: header (already has dark variants), mobile bottom nav, cards (.card-shadow/.card-elevated dark rules), inputs (dark inputs currently only set color, verify background/border contrast), and the customer dashboard.
Run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` in apps/web and fix any errors introduced.
acceptance_criteria: The theme selector is internally consistent: whatever option set (light/dark [+system]) is offered actually works end-to-end via next-themes with no dead 'System' option, and all toggle entry points behave identically.

The blanket `.dark .bg-white` / `.dark .text-slate-*` overrides no longer recolor elements meant to stay light; specifically the settings toggle knob and status dots remain visible in dark mode. Overrides are narrowed or replaced by token/dark: styling.

A `.dark ::selection` rule exists with legible contrast, and previously light-only components (language-switcher, web-onboarding, admin new-order-alert, collections-client, customer-requests-client, global-error) render correctly in dark mode.
Only one canonical theme-toggle implementation remains (or shared variants), with dark-aware mount placeholders; all import sites updated.
`npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` pass in apps/web with no new errors vs baseline.
verification: grep -rn "setTheme(\"system\")\|enableSystem" apps/web/components ; confirm consistency between providers and settings-client

findings.system_theme_decision: Chose STRICT LIGHT/DARK (removed the dead 'System' option) rather than enabling system support. Rationale: providers.tsx already ships enableSystem={false} + defaultTheme='light', and every toggle entry point already operated strictly light<->dark; only settings-client offered a dead 'System' ThemeOption calling setTheme('system'). Removing it (Appearance grid is now 2 columns: Light/Dark) is the lowest-risk change that makes the selector internally consistent. providers.tsx left unchanged (enableSystem stays false). All theme reads now use resolvedTheme for a single source of truth.
findings.toggle_consolidation: Consolidated three divergent toggles into one canonical file apps/web/components/theme-toggle.tsx exporting ThemeToggle (outline icon button), ThemeToggleIcon (circular animated glyph) and ThemeToggleInline (switch), all sharing one useThemeToggle() hook that reads resolvedTheme + a mounted guard. Deleted components/ui/theme-toggle.tsx and components/ui/theme-toggle-inline.tsx. Updated the only import site (app/account/page.tsx) to import ThemeToggleInline from '@/components/theme-toggle'. Fixed the inline mount placeholder to be dark-aware (bg-slate-100 dark:bg-slate-800) and the inactive track (bg-slate-200 dark:bg-slate-700).
findings.blanket_override_fix: In globals.css narrowed the blanket dark helpers to .dark .bg-white/.bg-slate-50/.border-slate-100/.border-slate-200/.text-slate-900/800/700 :not(.stay-light). These rules are kept as a pragmatic safety net (100 files / ~299 raw bg-white usages still rely on them; full removal would regress dark mode app-wide), but now intentional light-on-dark elements opt out with a `stay-light` class. Added `stay-light` to: settings-client toggle knob, ThemeToggleInline knob, and the white pulse/status dots in live-order-banner.tsx and live-order-tracking.tsx (they sit on colored gradients). Note: bg-white/12, bg-white/80, bg-white/90 opacity variants are separate Tailwind classes and were never hit by the blanket rule.
findings.selection_dark: Added `.dark ::selection { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.22); color: hsl(210 40% 98%); }` next to the existing light `::selection` (rgba(0,0,0,0.12)) which was nearly invisible on dark backgrounds.
findings.light_only_components: Added explicit dark styling: language-switcher.tsx (dark border/bg/text/hover/ring-offset); web-onboarding.tsx (white icon circle marked stay-light so the black brand icon stays visible on the colored gradient); admin/new-order-alert.tsx (card bg-white->dark:bg-neutral-900, orange header + accents get dark variants, dismiss button + body text dark variants); app/global-error.tsx (this replaces the root layout + ThemeProvider, so an inline pre-paint script now mirrors localStorage 'theme' / prefers-color-scheme onto documentElement and dark: variants added to body/card/button); admin/collections-client.tsx and admin/customer-requests-client.tsx converted bg-white->bg-card and border-slate-200->border-border (semantic tokens) plus dark: text variants.
findings.surfaces_audited: app/dashboard/page.tsx hero uses bg-black + white text which is correct in BOTH themes (intentional dark card), left as-is. app/admin/dispatch/page.tsx and app/admin/orders/[id]/page.tsx use raw bg-white/border-slate-200/text-slate-900 and render correctly via the preserved (narrowed) safety-net rules (none of their elements need stay-light), so no regression; left as-is. Header, mobile nav and cards already had dark variants / semantic tokens.
findings.verification: From apps/web: `npx prisma generate` then `npx tsc --noEmit` = 0 errors. `npm run lint` = 0 errors, 3 pre-existing warnings (unchanged from baseline). `npm test` = 61 pass / 2 fail; the 2 failures are exactly the pre-existing baseline ones (#47 order-checkout DELIVERY_OUT_OF_RANGE, #54 scroll-preservation touch-action) — no new failures. The globals.css edits (::selection dark rule + :not(.stay-light) narrowing) do not touch the html/body touch-action lines that test #54 inspects.
findings.artifacts: Reverted apps/web/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo (regenerated by tsc, tracked build cache) to keep the commit clean, matching FEAT-001's approach.

review_fixes_2026_07_01.issue_4_toggle_icon_convention: FIXED. In components/theme-toggle.tsx, ThemeToggleIcon now shows the TARGET state (the state you'd switch to), matching ThemeToggle: in dark mode it renders the Sun (switch to light) and in light mode the Moon (switch to dark). Previously it showed the current state (Moon in dark), which contradicted ThemeToggle within the same consolidated file. The framer-motion rotate/scale glyph animation and the shared useThemeToggle() hook are unchanged.
review_fixes_2026_07_01.issue_2_blanket_override_skipped: ACCEPTED FOLLOW-UP (not changed). The `.dark .bg-white:not(.stay-light)` (and sibling slate) blanket overrides remain as pragmatic temporary scaffolding: ~299 raw bg-white usages across ~100 files still depend on them, so full removal would regress dark mode app-wide and is out of scope for a low-risk review-fix pass. The real fix is the ongoing semantic-token migration (offers page, collections/customer-requests clients already migrated); the blanket rule should be retired as that migration completes.
review_fixes_2026_07_01.issue_3_back_nav_skipped: ACCEPTED FOLLOW-UP (not changed). components/header.tsx uses `window.history.length > 1` to decide router.back() vs the parent-path fallback; this best-effort heuristic can send Back off-site when an inner page is opened directly from an external link. A robust fix needs an in-app navigation-depth counter (e.g. tracking pushes in a context/store), which is a larger, riskier change touching navigation state beyond this UI review-fix scope. The parent-path fallback still exists for the length<=1 case, so the common in-app flow is correct. Left as a noted follow-up.
review_fixes_2026_07_01.verification: cd apps/web && npx prisma generate && npx tsc --noEmit = 0 errors; npm run lint = 0 errors + 3 pre-existing warnings; npm test = 61 pass / 2 fail where the 2 fails are exactly the baseline #47 (DELIVERY_OUT_OF_RANGE) and #54 (scroll-preservation) — no new failures. The toggle-icon change is JSX-only and does not touch CSS or tests.
